items.content: F05 (ideal-base W1): harden the background TaskStatusStore and build the verification matrix. Repo /Users/jrudnik/labs/jcode, branch main. READ FIRST: docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F04/README.md, the full F04 review reviews/F04-implementation-review.md (especially the 10-item F05 handoff list in Round 3), crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs, crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs, and WORK_GRAPH.json node F05. OWNED PATHS (write ONLY these): crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs, crates/jcode-base/src/background/tests.rs, crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs, docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/**. WORK: (1) Crash durability: fsync the temp file before rename and fsync the parent directory after rename in write_atomic (handoff item 1); correct the doc comment. (2) Stale temp cleanup: sweep *.json.tmp.* leftovers (dead-PID owned or age-based) during reconcile_orphaned_tasks (item 2). (3) Task-ID collision policy: document and test write_initial semantics for an existing Running file (item 4). (4) Verification fixtures for the F05 gates: (a) concurrency matrix test - concurrent progress/delivery/completion from MULTIPLE BackgroundTaskManager instances sharing one directory (cross-instance, not just one store) never tears JSON or overwrites terminal truth; (b) crash-interruption fixture - simulate a torn write by planting a *.json.tmp.* file plus a valid old status file, prove readers see the old state and reconciliation cleans the temp; (c) malformed-file matrix - truncated JSON, empty file, wrong schema: startup reconciliation (reconcile_orphaned_tasks + list/status paths) must surface them without false success and without crashing; (d) orphan reconciliation and cleanup re-verified after your changes. (5) Run scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-base --lib (all must pass) and -p jcode-app-core --lib server:: (spot check). (6) Write bounded evidence to docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/README.md (+SHA256SUMS) describing each gate with test names. (7) Commit your exact owned paths only, message starting 'F05: '. DO NOT expand this node or spawn workers; do the work directly. DO NOT touch docs/fork/recovery or normalization. Complete with a typed artifact: findings, evidence (file:line + commit sha), edge_cases_considered, validation (exact commands + counts), open_questions, confidence, what_i_did_not_check.

F05 independent verification review (ideal-base W1). Repo /Users/jrudnik/labs/jcode, branch main. You are an independent adversarial reviewer; your job is to find real gaps. READ: the F05 implementation commit(s) (git log --oneline -8, git show the F05 commits), docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/README.md, crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs, background.rs, background/tests.rs, plus WORK_GRAPH.json node F05 acceptance gates: (1) 'Concurrent progress/delivery/completion never tears JSON or overwrites terminal truth', (2) 'Startup reconciles malformed/orphaned state without false success'. VERIFY adversarially: run scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-base --lib yourself; audit the fsync/rename durability claim against the actual code; check the cross-instance concurrency test actually uses multiple manager instances; probe the malformed-file matrix for false-success paths (a malformed file silently treated as absent anywhere reachable from list/status/wait/reconcile); check stale-temp sweep correctness (cannot delete a live writer's temp). Also check the F04 Round-3 handoff items 1,2,4 were addressed and items deferred are named honestly. WRITE docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F05-verification-review.md naming the exact commit reviewed AND the model you actually ran as. Structure: Verdict (PASS only if both gates honestly met, else FAIL with exact citations), Validation performed, Findings (blocking/important/minor), Gate checklist, What I did not check, Confidence. Commit ONLY that file, message 'docs(ideal-base): F05 independent verification review'. DO NOT expand this node. DO NOT modify any other file. Complete with a typed artifact including your verdict in findings.

Final plan-wide critique: audit the ENTIRE plan adversarially before it may finish. Read every completed node's artifact, especially each 'what_i_did_not_check' and every open question, and hunt for whole facets the plan never covered. For each gap, inject a new node; the plan cannot finish until they drain.

Independently identify the exact F05 implementation commit(s) from git log -8 and git show. Audit touched files, commit parent/range, worktree state, and record exact SHA plus relevant diffs/citations. Do not modify files.

Run the required `scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-base --lib` independently. Record exact command, exit status, pass/fail/ignored counts, duration if visible, and any flakes or warnings. Do not modify files.

Adversarially audit crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs fsync/temp/rename/parent-fsync implementation. Determine exactly what durability and atomicity it guarantees and does not guarantee, including platform behavior and error paths. Cite exact lines. Do not modify files.

Audit status mutation concurrency and terminal truth preservation across store.rs/background.rs. Look for lost updates, read-modify-write races, lock registry scope, poison/error behavior, and terminal overwrites. Cite exact lines and classify gate impact. Do not modify files.

Audit and run the cross-instance concurrency test(s). Verify they construct multiple independent BackgroundTaskManager and/or TaskStatusStore instances, share the same task path, continuously parse JSON, race progress/delivery/completion, and assert terminal truth. Identify coverage gaps such as cross-process behavior. Do not modify files.

Trace every reachable malformed-status path from startup reconcile, list, status, wait, and lower-level reads. Probe zero-byte, truncated JSON, wrong-schema valid JSON, and malformed collision behavior for false-success or silent-absence outcomes. Run targeted tests or ephemeral external probes, but do not modify repository files. Cite exact lines.

Adversarially audit stale-temp sweep parsing and deletion conditions. Determine whether it can delete a live writer temp under PID reuse, long writes, age threshold, naming/path quirks, races, or platform process checks. Run targeted existing tests. Cite exact lines and gate impact. Do not modify files.

Audit startup orphan reconciliation semantics in background.rs and tests. Check dead/current/live-foreign/legacy/detached/terminal/malformed cases and look specifically for false success. Cite exact lines. Do not modify files.

Locate the F04 Round-3 handoff/review and independently verify items 1, 2, and 4 were addressed in F05. Identify all deferred items and assess whether F05 evidence names them honestly. Cite source review and implementation/evidence lines. Do not modify files.

Audit docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/README.md and SHA256 manifest against actual code/tests and WORK_GRAPH.json F05 acceptance gates. Flag overclaims, missing qualifications, or dishonest deferrals. Verify hashes. Do not modify files.
Read WORK_GRAPH.json node F05 and independently map every acceptance gate to concrete implementation and tests. Produce a strict PASS/FAIL recommendation with blocking citations. Do not modify files.

Perform a fresh red-team review of the F05 commit focusing on any real gap not explicitly called out in the prompt. Inspect relevant code/tests and run focused commands as needed. Do not modify files.

Critique the work of 'f05-review' adversarially. Read every child's 'what_i_did_not_check' and find unexplored gaps given this task's stated scope. For each gap, emit a new child node; do not pass until no gaps remain.
Independently inspect git log -8 and git show metadata/messages to identify the exact F05 implementation commit SHA(s). Do not modify files. Report exact commands/output citations and rationale.
Audit the identified or likely F05 commit parent(s), ancestry, and exact comparison range. Inspect git log/show/rev-parse. Do not modify files. Report exact SHAs and command evidence.
Audit files touched by likely F05 implementation commit(s), including name-status/stat and whether changes are confined to expected scope. Do not modify files. Report exact paths and evidence.
Independently inspect full relevant diffs for likely F05 implementation commit(s). Summarize substantive changes and provide exact diff hunk/file-line citations. Do not modify files.
Audit current worktree/index/untracked state and determine whether it affects attribution of F05 implementation changes. Use status/diff commands only. Do not modify files. Report exact evidence.

Cross-check git log -8 commit sequence for adjacent commits that may be part of F05, including split implementation, test, or fix commits. Inspect commit messages and diffs independently. Do not modify files. Report inclusion/exclusion rationale and exact SHAs.

Critique the work of 'f05-review-commit-scope' adversarially. Read every child's 'what_i_did_not_check' and find unexplored gaps given this task's stated scope. For each gap, emit a new child node; do not pass until no gaps remain.

Independently capture `git log -8` in exact newest-to-oldest order, including full SHAs, subjects, bodies, authors/dates, and parent relationships. Flag message-level indications of F05 or split implementation/test/fix adjacency. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~1 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~2 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~3 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~4 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~5 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~6 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.
Inspect HEAD~7 independently: full commit message and complete diff/stat versus first parent. Determine whether it belongs to F05, with concrete rationale and exact SHA. Do not modify files.

Independently inspect the combined patch series across `git log -8` and identify adjacent commits that form one feature through implementation/test/fix/refactor splits. Focus on F05 scope boundaries and cite exact SHAs and changed paths. Do not modify files.

Critique the work of 'f05-crosscheck-scope' adversarially. Read every child's 'what_i_did_not_check' and find unexplored gaps given this task's stated scope. For each gap, emit a new child node; do not pass until no gaps remain.

f05-harden-verify.artifact_json: {"findings":"Implemented F05 hardening in commit 9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe. Status writes now fsync the unique temp file before rename and fsync the parent directory afterward. Independent manager/store instances in one process serialize by final path. Startup reconciliation removes dead-PID or 24-hour stale temp siblings. write_initial now rejects any existing valid status, including Running, and surfaces malformed collisions. The verification matrix covers cross-instance racing progress/delivery/completion with continuous JSON parsing, crash-interruption old-state visibility, stale cleanup, malformed startup/list/status behavior, collisions, and orphan regressions. The matrix exposed F04-R3-I1, so terminal construction now preserves freshly persisted delivery flags. Evidence and SHA256 manifest are committed, and the worktree is clean.","evidence":["9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs:68-145 process-wide path locks and temp-fsync/rename/parent-fsync durability","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs:148-192 dead-PID/24-hour stale temp cleanup","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs:228-245 create-once write_initial collision policy","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/store.rs:585-600 existing Running collision test","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs:422-456 startup temp cleanup and orphan reconciliation","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs:1672-1711 persisted delivery flags preserved in terminal construction","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/tests.rs:633-751 cross-instance concurrency matrix","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe crates/jcode-base/src/background/tests.rs:754-868 crash interruption, dead/aged temp cleanup, and malformed-file matrix","9f4d34d11e9c54e8023538d6bb4ceff0780f0dfe docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/README.md:1-50 bounded gate ledger; SHA256SUMS verified"],"edge_cases_considered":["Independent BackgroundTaskManager/TaskStatusStore instances in one process sharing the same directory and task path","Same-process temp-name collisions and PID reuse, avoided with PID plus UUID and create_new","Dead-PID temp leftovers and age-expired leftovers whose encoded PID is still live","Truncated JSON, zero-byte files, and syntactically valid wrong-schema JSON","Existing Running and terminal task-ID collisions, plus malformed existing files","Progress and delivery mutations racing natural terminal completion while a raw reader continuously parses the final file","Delivery updates persisted immediately before terminal construction or during terminal retry recovery","Reload-owned and dead-process orphans versus current-instance, live foreign, legacy ownerless, and detached statuses","Terminal persistence outage tombstones, recovery pruning, and cancel recovery"],"validation":"Exact commands: `scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-base --lib background` => 43 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored, 1146 filtered out. `scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-base --lib` => 1186 passed, 0 failed, 3 ignored. `scripts/dev_cargo.sh test -p jcode-app-core --lib server::` first run => 467 passed, 1 flaky unrelated dead-PID sweep failure; isolated exact rerun => 1 passed; full server-filter rerun => 468 passed, 0 failed, 681 filtered out. `git diff --check` => exit 0. `shasum -a 256 -c docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F05/SHA256SUMS` => README.md OK. Commit path census => exactly the 5 owned files; final `git status --short` empty.","open_questions":["Whether a future node should add true cross-process same-task advisory locking rather than the current process-wide registry","Whether Windows rename-over-existing and directory fsync behavior should receive a platform-specific implementation and fixture","Whether the global path-lock registry should reclaim entries for very long-lived high-cardinality processes"],"confidence":"high","what_i_did_not_check":["A real kernel crash, host power loss, disk-full condition, or external filesystem fault injector","True cross-process writers contending on the same task ID","Windows replace-over-existing semantics and Windows directory durability","Miri, Loom, sanitizers, or the full workspace outside the requested suites","Long-duration measurement of process-wide lock-map growth"]}
